name: Kazakhstan (Republic of Kazakhstan / Қазақстан Республикасы)
summary: Kazakhstan is a Central Asian civil-law unitary republic whose family-law framework operates under the Code on Marriage (Matrimony) and Family of 26 December 2011 (No. 518-IV) — federal civil-code framework drawing on post-Soviet civil-law tradition. Parental rights and child custody are governed by Code arts. 60-77. The Supreme Court of Kazakhstan (Қазақстан Республикасының Жоғарғы Соты) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (restructured 2023 from Constitutional Council) operates constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in District/City Specialised Inter-District Court for Civil Cases. Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Healthcare framework. Kazakhstan is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the child's-best-interests standard codified in Code art. 61. Kazakhstan acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 September 2013.

statutory_framework.citation: Code on Marriage (Matrimony) and Family 2011 (No. 518-IV) arts. 60-77
statutory_framework.title: Code on Marriage and Family — Parental rights and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Code on Marriage and Family enacted replacing 1998 Marriage and Family Law. Arts. 60-77 govern parental rights and child custody.

statutory_framework.citation: Law on Rights of the Child 345-II of 2002
statutory_framework.title: Law on Rights of the Child
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal children's rights statute aligned with UNCRC obligations.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court (Жоғарғы Сот)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review (restructured 2023 from Constitutional Council).

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Healthcare, Kazakhstan
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Kazakhstani family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Kazakh SSR + Soviet-republic framework
key_developments.description: Kazakh Soviet Socialist Republic established 5 December 1936 — substantively distinctive Central Asian Soviet-republic framework. Subsequent 1991 declaration of independence following Soviet collapse. Foundational pre-1991-independence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Kazakhstan independence + Nazarbayev-presidency-+-Soviet-collapse framework
key_developments.description: Kazakhstan declared independence 16 December 1991 — substantively distinctive Central Asian last-Soviet-republic-to-declare-independence framework. Foundational substantive Nursultan Nazarbayev presidency 1991-2019 framework + 30+ year independent-republic framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Kazakhstan ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + UNCRC-applicability framework
key_developments.description: Kazakhstan ratified UNCRC 12 August 1994 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2002 Law on Rights of the Child + 2011 Code on Marriage and Family substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Constitution of Kazakhstan + post-Soviet-constitutional-framework framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of Kazakhstan adopted by referendum 30 August 1995 — substantively distinctive Central Asian post-Soviet-constitutional-anchor framework with Constitutional Council (restructured 2023 to Constitutional Court). Foundational substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: Law on Marriage and Family + post-Soviet-family-law-codification framework
key_developments.description: Federal Law on Marriage and Family 1998 enacted post-Soviet — substantively significant Central Asian post-Soviet-family-law-codification framework. Foundational pre-2011-Code-on-Marriage-and-Family framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Law on Rights of the Child 345-II + UNCRC-aligned framework
key_developments.description: Federal Law on Rights of the Child 345-II 2002 enacted aligned with UNCRC obligations — substantively significant Central Asian child-rights framework. Foundational substantive child-rights framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Code on Marriage (Matrimony) and Family + No-518-IV-comprehensive-codification framework
key_developments.description: Federal Code on Marriage (Matrimony) and Family No. 518-IV 2011 enacted replacing 1998 statute codifying marriage, parental rights (arts. 60-77), and child custody — substantively significant Central Asian comprehensive-family-law-codification framework. Foundational substantive family-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession + Central-Asian-Hague-cluster framework
key_developments.description: Kazakhstan acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 September 2013 — substantively significant Central Asian Hague-accession framework. Substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Bloody-January-2022-protests + Tokayev-presidency-+-post-Nazarbayev framework
key_developments.description: Bloody January 2022 protests 2-11 January 2022 substantively reshaping Kazakh-political framework — substantively distinctive Central Asian post-Nazarbayev-2019-transition + Tokayev-presidency framework. Subsequent constitutional reforms removing references to Nazarbayev affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Domestic Violence Law + Saltanat Nukenova case + family-law-modernisation framework
key_developments.description: Kazakhstan Domestic Violence Law amendments April 2024 criminalising domestic violence following Saltanat Nukenova case + former minister Kuandyk Bishimbayev conviction May 2024 — substantively distinctive globally only modern Central Asian celebrity-domestic-violence-trial-precipitated criminal-law-reform framework + structurally distinctive post-Nazarbayev-era Kazakh women's-rights-reform framework + substantively significant institutional context affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-implementation under Code on Marriage and Family 2011 + Supreme Court substantive register evolution + Hague Convention 1980 cross-border implementation framework + foundational post-Saltanat-Nukenova-era domestic-violence + family-law-implementation framework.

key_developments.title: Constitutional Court restructure + Bota Janet KZN children's-rights framework + post-Nazarbayev-amendments
key_developments.description: Constitutional Court of Kazakhstan restructured 2023 from Constitutional Council with substantive original constitutional-review jurisdiction. Children's Rights Ombudsperson institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Code on Marriage and Family 2011 + Law on Rights of the Child 2002 framework. Post-Nazarbayev constitutional amendments 2022 substantively reshaping institutional context. Substantively significant Central Asian institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court of Kazakhstan + Constitutional Court — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + continuing-Tokayev-+-restructured-Constitutional-Court framework
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of Kazakhstan (Қазақстан Республикасының Жоғарғы Соты) and Constitutional Court (restructured 2023 from Constitutional Council) continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Code on Marriage and Family 2011 arts. 60-77 + Law on Rights of the Child 2002 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within continuing Kassym-Jomart Tokayev presidency since 2019. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Kazakhstan operates a post-Soviet civil-law framework — Code on Marriage and Family 2011 reflects post-Soviet codification trajectory shared with Russia, Belarus, Ukraine within the corpus.
Hague Convention 1980 accession 2013 places Kazakhstan in the Hague Central Asian cluster.
Bilingual official-language framework (Kazakh + Russian) reflects post-Soviet language-policy heritage.
editorial_notes: Kazakhstan jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 5 to 10 key_developments with full Kazakh-SSR-1936-to-Tokayev-presidency trajectory: 1936-Kazakh-SSR + 1991-independence-+-Nazarbayev-presidency + 1994-Kazakhstan-UNCRC-ratification + 1995-Constitution-of-Kazakhstan + 1998-Law-on-Marriage-and-Family + 2002-Law-on-Rights-of-the-Child + 2011-Code-on-Marriage-and-Family-518-IV + 2013-Hague-Convention-1980-accession + 2022-Bloody-January-2022-+-Tokayev-presidency + 2024-Supreme-Court-+-Constitutional-Court-restructured-best-interests.

Civil-law post-Soviet Central Asia (Code on Marriage and Family 2011 + Law on Rights of the Child 2002 + Hague Convention 1980 accession 2013 + bilingual Kazakh/Russian + Constitutional Court 2023 restructure). Last Soviet republic to declare independence 1991 + 30+ year post-Soviet independent-republic framework.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Code on Marriage and Family 2011 arts. 60-77 + Law on Rights of the Child 2002 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Central-Asian + civil-law + post-Soviet-republic-cluster + last-Soviet-republic-to-declare-independence-1991-distinctive + Kazakh-SSR-1936 + Nazarbayev-presidency-1991-2019 + Tokayev-presidency-since-2019-+-Bloody-January-2022-protests + Constitution-of-Kazakhstan-1995-+-Constitutional-Council-+-Constitutional-Court-2023-restructure + Code-on-Marriage-and-Family-2011-No-518-IV + Law-on-Rights-of-the-Child-2002 + bilingual-Kazakh-Russian-post-Soviet-language-policy + Hague-Convention-accession-2013 + post-Soviet-codification-trajectory-with-Russia-Belarus-Ukraine clusters within the corpus.
